course_title: Communication and Swallowing Across the Lifespan II

learning_outcomes.description: Apply foundational frameworks and principles to support ethical, person-centred, and evidence-informed adult speech pathology practice and advocacy. (SPA PS Domains/Standards 1.1, 1.6, 1.7, 2.5, 3.1)
Explain and apply key theories of language, speech processing, and cognition in adults to inform clinical reasoning and communication strategies. (SPA PS Domains/Standards 2.5, 3.1, 3.3)
Describe the components and functional implications of communication and swallowing in typically ageing adults. (SPA PS Domains/Standards 3.1, 3.2)

Describe key theoretical models and concepts related to adult communication and swallowing disorders, including aphasia, motor speech disorders, cognitive-communication impairments, and dysphagia, to support clinical reasoning. (SPA PS Domains/Standards 2.5)

Describe the professional role of a speech pathologist in adult settings, including responsibilities in advocacy, education, and interprofessional collaboration. (SPA PS Domains/Standards 1.4, 1.7, 3.1, 3.6)

Demonstrate professional and ethical written communication skills for public-facing and clinical contexts, with consideration for advocacy of the profession and the needs of adults with communication and swallowing impairments. (SPA PS Domains/Standards 1.5, 1.7, 3.1, 3.6)
Reflect on and apply learning related to communication and swallowing in adults to support professional growth and evidence-informed practice. (SPA PS Domains/Standards 2.1, 2.2, 2.5)
Work effectively in professional teams (SPA PS Domains/Standards 1.4, 2.3, 2.5, 2.6, 3.1, 3.3, 3.4).

assessments.task_description:
As part of Speech Pathology Week, students will create a 1–2 minute Instagram reel for the UQHealth social media account. The reel must promote the speech pathology profession or studying speech pathology at UQ and be tailored to an audience of current and prospective health students. The content must also align with the official theme of Speech Pathology Australia (SPA) Week and reflect the tone and values of the UQHealth brand.

Students will present their reels during tutorials in week 5. The multimedia and peer mark for the social media campaign are due by 2pm Monday 17th August, 2026 (Week 4).

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

When a student is required to complete an oral or practical assessment item worth 20% or more, the performance will be recorded and retained per Appendix 1. Assessment Procedure (95)
assessments.submission_guidelines: The assessment task will be submitted via Blackboard Assignments
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
• Reflection 1 ( Pre-simulation): Due 2 pm on Thursday 3rd September , 2026 
  • Reflection 2 (Post Simulation 1): Due 2 pm on Thursday 10th September, 2026 
  • Reflection 3 (Post Simulation 2): Due 2 pm on Thursday 17th September , 2026
assessments.task_description:
Reflective practice is a core component of the speech pathology profession and underpins excellence in the profession. Students are required to submit a series of Reflective Practice Statements. These reflections will be informed by participation in a tutorial activity and two simulated clinical encounters, in which students will practise skills in clinical reasoning, effective communication, lifelong learning, and professionalism. Students will attend the tutorial in week 6, and simulated practicals in week 7 and 8. Absence from sessions in weeks 7 and 8 requires a medical certificate to be submitted to the Course Coordinator.

Students will complete a series of three (3) reflective practice statements on their skills, knowledge, and learning gained from the simulation sessions, which will count 15% (5% per reflection) towards the overall grade in this course. Prompt questions will be provided to guide the reflection.

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The assessment task will be submitted via TurnItIn
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
Students will participate in an adult case history simulation with a standardised patient in week 8. Following the practical, students will submit a clinical progress note based on the session.

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The assessment task will be submitted via Turnitin on Blackboard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
The end-of-semester examination will assess content from your lectures, online learnings, tutorial content, and required readings from weeks 1-12 of semester. The end-of-semester examination counts 40% of the total assessment for this course and is not must pass.

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 12(27 Jul - 25 Oct)
learning_activities.topic:
On-Campus Tutorials

Tutorials will be held in person on campus during weeks 1-6 and weeks 9-12 of the semeter. Each session will run for 2 hours and incorporate small group discussions to explore key concepts in greater depth. These sessions are designed as experiential learning activities and therefore students are expected to attend and actively engage. To prepare, students must complete the associated online self-paced learning modules and preparation tasks before each tutorial. As these sessions involve small group work, they will not be recorded. 


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06, L07, L08

learning_activities.topic:
Online Self-Paced Learning

This weekly series of online modules is designed for self-paced learning. It is expected that this content will have been completed prior to attending the in-person tutorial each week. More information about the topics covered each week is available on Blackboard Ultra course site.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06, L07

learning_activities.topic:
Week 1 Lecture

The first lecture will provide an introduction to the course and creating information on creating a social media post.

learning_activities.topic:
Practical Series

In week 7 and 8, students will engage in an adult case history simulation clinic in the acute hospital environment. These practicals are held in-person at the St Lucia campus.

This simulation provides students with scaffolded, experiential learning opportunities to develop and demonstrate professional communication and information-gathering skills in adult practice. Students will work in pairs and be supervised by a Practice Educator who will provide constructive feedback.

These practicals will not be recorded.

Learning outcomes: L01, L03, L04, L06, L07, L08
